response.justification: The candidate is a skilled full-stack web developer with broad experience across TypeScript, Node.js, and various web technologies, but falls critically short of the core requirements for this role. The position demands 8 years of C++ experience as a minimum qualification; the candidate lists only roughly 6 months of C/C++ work at National Instruments and a bachelor thesis project using C++, with C++ not even appearing in the skills section. There is no evidence of cloud compute platform work, systems-level infrastructure ownership at scale, or formal technical leadership of engineering teams. The candidate's strongest systems-adjacent experience—low-latency microservices at Dunnhumby—is relevant but insufficient to bridge the gap to a cloud compute platform leadership role.
strengths: Demonstrated experience with high-throughput distributed systems (millions of HTTP requests/sec at Dunnhumby) shows some systems-level thinking
Active open-source contributor and community educator (workshops, talks, GISCollective) indicating ability to share knowledge and mentor others
Polyglot background spanning embedded, web, mobile, and infrastructure tooling shows adaptability across problem domains
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months) against the 8-year minimum requirement — a disqualifying gap for a C++ platform lead role
No cloud compute platform, large-scale infrastructure, or distributed systems architecture ownership at the depth or tenure required (5 years minimum)
No formal technical leadership role; current and past titles are individual contributor (Senior/Staff Developer), with no evidence of owning team-level technical strategy or roadmap
